entry.title: Re: TM - Public cloud - Location geocoordinates
entry.content:
Good day @jignesh_parikh 

Thank you for your question.

Yes, in the scenario you described you can maintain latitude and longitude manually for locations. If you consider doing it manually, that can be done in the Manage Locations app, and you do not require the HANA spatial services. When you manually maintain, the Calculated Geocoordinates function is disabled.

Here are references for more guidance

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/61e246f4b34c4e1790d8b7651c0b40a8/1cdd4e9bd51d468a97978df6b3a9a00c.html?locale=en-US

3709474 - Unable to Find Airports or Ports and Create new Locations in Manage Locations app - SAP for Me

 

So, in the Manage Locations app,

use the Geocoordinates tab to maintain latitude and longitude manually. Then save the location.

 

If your requirement is only to have geocoordinates available so that distance calculation can work with the location master data you maintained, then this manual maintenance is the first step to carry out.

However, if your customer wants automated geocoding, routing, or provider-based map and distances services, then HANA spatial services is the relevant SAP BTP service.

Regarding charges and plan, see the following:

https://help.sap.com/doc/842f6fbf036d489caca610c697c9d612/Cloud/en-US/cloud_SAP_HANA_Spatial_Services_Reference_en.pdf

 

So, in summary:

  • Manual latitude/longitude entry is supported in Manage Location. You do not require spatial services for that.
  • Automated geocoding, routing, location services require HANA spatial services on BTP. This is based on your consumption.

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

🔹Current Setup

    • Deployed and integrated it with SAP Build Work Zone

  • Subaccount B

entry.title: Re: Enable ssl in Hana replication for DR
entry.content:
Hello ,

SSL is enabled by default through parameter : "global.ini > [system_replication_communication] > enable_ssl" as per the help guide page : Configure Secure Communication (TLS/SSL) Between Primary and Secondary Sites | SAP Help Portal

"TLS/SSL is enabled for replication source and target systems"

Kind Regards
Simon

entry.title: India Withholding Tax - Maintenance of New Income Type Including Section 393 - Effective 1st April 2
entry.content:
Due to the introduction of the Income Tax Act, 2025, all non-salary withholding tax sections (194, 195, 196 series) will be consolidated into a new TDS section, Section 393, effective 1st April 2026.

Additionally, section names have been changed to 1001, 1002, but updating these in the system is not possible due to character limits.

Based on these changes, TDS certificates are also being generated accordingly. Could you please clarify how these updates will impact reporting and file generation?

Kindly provide guidance on how to implement these changes in SAP Business One.

SAP Business One

entry.title: Re: Vendor Open Item Not Clearing
entry.content: The error means the vendor open item cannot be cleared because the asset side of the posting is missing or inconsistent. Please check the PO’s account assignment, the asset master linkage, and whether the MIRO/MIGO postings created the required asset line items. If the asset postings are missing, you may need to adjust the invoice or repost with the correct asset assignment so FI and AA are reconciled.

entry.title: Dispute Case Creation and Closure using 'Reprocess Bank Statement items Fiori app'
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,

I am facing an issue while processing incoming payments against an open item for which a dispute case already exists.

When I post the incoming payment using the Reprocess Bank Statement Items Fiori app, the open item gets cleared successfully. However, the paid amount is not updated in the dispute case (in UDM_DISPUTE/Manage Dispute Cases Fiori app), and the status does not change to Closed. Additionally, manual status updates are not possible, as the paid amount is not reflected and the dispute amount does not reduce to zero.

In contrast, when I perform the same end-to-end process using F-28 (Process Incoming Payments), the dispute case is updated correctly and is automatically closed.

Could you please help me understand why this difference in behavior occurs when using the Reprocess Bank Statement Items app? Is the integration with Dispute Management limited or handled differently in this app?

I have reviewed relevant SAP Notes and blogs but could not find a solution specific to this scenario.

Any guidance would be greatly appreciated. Please let me know if any additional details are required.

Thank you!

#Dispute Case #Reprocess Bank Statement items Fiori app #Dispute Case Closure #Residual item #UDM_DISPUTE #Manage Dispute Cases #FSCM #Financial supply chain Management

entry.title: Re: SAP RDP: ES Plastic Tax - Missing Tax ID for delivier...
entry.content:
If the logistic process for the EU Acquisition is Stock Transfer Import, then the tax number is taken from the sending plant. RDP has a screen in the Organization Data app, which allows maintenance of tax numbers for plants.

If the logistic process for the EU Acquisition is "Delivery as import", then from what I see, RDP takes the customer's tax number, not the sending plant's tax number.

Check which logistic process is involved - stock transfers or delivery as import.

From your explanation it seems that it is delivery as import.

Check if the customer of these deliveries has a tax number.

entry.title: Chane of E Mail Subject while submitting a document
entry.content:
The Email Subject can be changes before the initial release of a document.

if you want to resubmit that document you can also change the subject.

Since only the Initial output can be resubmitted there is no feature  hardcode the last resubmitted email Subject, changes .

 

Any resubmission will take the Subject from the Initial output but it is modifiable

entry.title: Re: CK11N difference prod version between main product an...
entry.content: Use CK40N (mass costing) with production version explicitly assigned. Or temporarily adjust production version determination in customizing (transaction OPL8 / production scheduling profile). Since you already confirmed SAP note is not suitable to your version, raise an OSS incident referencing Note 3356710 and your release level. SAP typically provides a correction note or transport for the newer release.

entry.title: Re: How to do background scheduling with clean core, including Fiori apps, replacing se38 reports
entry.content:
Hi @MaximilianoDestro 

Sorry, I missed this comment until now 😞

There is no "callback" once an application job has completed, so it wouldn't be possible like that. But, in one of the comments higher up I am explaining how to have "multiple steps". So your requirement could work like this:

  1. Create a new application job template for each "other process" you need to run. For simpleness sake, let's say we have 1 and we call it "ZAPJ_START_PROCESS01"
  2. Create a new "main" application job template in the Fiori app Application Jobs. Choose the standard one that you want to run first. Then go to "Maintain Steps" and then choose ZAPJ_START_PROCESS01. Repeat the maintain steps until all your processes are done
  3. Give the new "main" application job template a name
  4. Now when your API is called, instead of starting the standard job with CL_APJ_RT_API=>SCHEDULE_JOB, you start this new "main" template. It should then execute step after step, one after the other is completed.

Hope that helps and I got your requirement right.

BR

entry.title: Re: Standard reports/apps in SAP Public Cloud 2602 for SA...
entry.content:
Dear @tushar_sonawane53,

1. Overview / dashboard apps

  • Order to Cash Dashboard overview of key order-to-cash KPIs such as sales volume, profit margin and incoming orders
  • Sales Management Overview graphical management overview of sales data
  • My Sales Overview dashboard-style overview for day-to-day sales monitoring
  • Customer 360° View customer-centric view of related sales business documents
  • Customer Returns Overview overview of return KPIs and return trends

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a376cd9ea00d476b96f18dea1247e6a5/c4cce03194104a0184bd2435d9697be6.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a376cd9ea00d476b96f18dea1247e6a5/9afe5824a6dd4de0a80c82fbd7ad5dc2.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a376cd9ea00d476b96f18dea1247e6a5/7cafd9570785c024e10000000a44147b.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a376cd9ea00d476b96f18dea1247e6a5/ed46ef43a0c145bca2db07f438b66f57.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a376cd9ea00d476b96f18dea1247e6a5/08843981e138402f9db3e2c48dfa2506.html

2. Standard analytical reports

  • Sales Quotations Flexible Analysis standard quotation analysis for flexible business dimensions
  • Quotation Conversion Rates Valid/Not Completed quotation-to-order conversion analysis
  • Incoming Sales Orders Flexible Analysis incoming sales order trend analysis
  • Sales Volume Flexible Analysis sales volume and billing KPI trend analysis
  • Sales Volume Detailed Analysis detailed drilldown of invoiced sales by business dimensions
  • Sales Volume Check Open Sales analysis of sales volume together with open orders and open deliveries
  • Sales Volume Profit Margin analysis of sales volume versus profit margin
  • Sales Performance Plan/Actual plan-versus-actual sales analysis
  • Sales Performance Predictions predictive sales performance analysis
  • Customer Returns Flexible Analysis returns trend and return-rate analysis
  • Delivery Performance monitoring of current delivery performance for sales orders
  • Sales Order Items Backorders analysis of confirmed orders, backorders and demand fulfillment

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a376cd9ea00d476b96f18dea1247e6a5/e846f3b7c95a40c2b24c393f942a2855.html

3. Operational monitoring / execution apps

  • Manage Sales Quotations Version 2 operational worklist for sales quotations
  • Manage Sales Orders Version 2 central worklist for sales orders
  • Track Sales Orders end-to-end tracking of sales order fulfillment status
  • Track Sales Order Details detailed process-flow and issue view for a sales order

4. Complementary standard apps

  • Manage Sales Plans maintenance of sales targets used together with plan/actual reporting

entry.title: Re: Account Determination MM - How to deactivate rules
entry.content: Hi Max. I currently dont have access to a public cloud system so I can’t say for sure, but as far as I remember certain configuration settings in MM account determination are restricted once they are activated, especially when they are part of the predefined scope. In many cases, these rules cannot be deactivated again via configuration, even if no valuation class entries have been maintained yet. This is due to the cloud system design, where some settings are fixed to ensure consistency and stability of financial postings. A common misunderstanding is to treat these switches like flexible customizing options from on-premise systems. In Public Cloud, the system enforces stricter controls and does not always allow reverting such changes. In practice, this means that account determination needs to be completed for all relevant valuation classes once the rule is active. A practical approach is to carefully review and plan account determination settings before activation, and ensure that required valuation classes and accounts are defined upfront to avoid follow-up issues. Hope it was helpful. Many greetings Frank 😊

entry.title: SAPGUI for Windows frontend printing does not send data to print server after first print
entry.content:
Hello,

we are facing an issue where some users are no longer able to use the frontend printing after replacing their printer. 

The default printer of the users was upgraded to a newer model which is installed via a Windows print server. Printing via Windows works fine for the users. However, when printing using SAPGUI and a front end printer the first print is printed fine, but any additionally print will be stuck sending to the print server. We see that a spool file is created in the spool of the printer, but the print server receives no data.
When the user closes SAPGUI, the spool file is deleted and after reopening SAPGUI, the same error appears: The first print works fine, the second one is "stuck".

We are using "I2SWIN : MS Windows driver, Latin-2" as device class as the printer has to print some special characters of Easter European languages. We already updated the SAPGUI version to the lastest PL, delete the cache in SAPGUI as well as its temporary files.

Does anybody knows how to fix this issue or where to look further ?

Windows 11
SAPGUI 8 PL 14 and 16 (64-Bit)

entry.title: SAP TM Public Cloud: Is Round‑Trip Transportation with Empty Legs Supported?
entry.content:
Hi experts,

I am currently supporting a large SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition implementation in which Transportation Management is a core component of daily inbound and outbound operations. I would appreciate peer guidance on a specific transportation planning scenario.

Business context

Our logistics model relies on land vehicles operating in continuous round-trip cycles:

  • An empty truck travels from Location A to Location B to collect material.
  • A full truckload then transports material from Location B to Location A, after which the cycle repeats. 

Question

From a business perspective, this should ideally be represented as a single round-trip execution. In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition TM, I would therefore like to model a Freight Order with:

  • an empty leg (A → B) for collection
  • a loaded leg (B → A) for transportation 

However, this does not seem to be supported in Cloud Public.

In Private Cloud implementations, I have seen similar scenarios handled using Freight Order Building Rules to enable the planning of empty movements, together with Continuous Move optimisation to link loaded and empty stages into a single round‑trip execution.

We are operating strictly within SAP standard, using delivered Fiori applications (Transportation Cockpit, Manage Freight Orders…), and are deliberately avoiding custom developments.

Is this scenario currently outside the functional scope of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition TM, or is there a standard modelling approach recommended by SAP that I may be overlooking?

Any insights based on practical implementation experience would be highly appreciated.

Kind regards,

entry.title: S/4 HANA How to Get rid of Basic Auth - SOAMANAGER Local Configuration on Client Certificate
entry.content:
The local configuration in SOAMANAGER is also called automatic configuration. Rather than configured manully, the logical port is configured by the task automatically in the background.

However, in this mode, the authentication doesn't support OAuth2. To get rid of basic auth, an option is to use client certificate.

Configuration Steps

let's take sales order notification outbound as an example.

This is the office help document:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/19d48293097f4a2589433856b034dfa5/ed66f965148548a9bb76c24be640d7fc.html?locale=en-US

1. Generate certificate files and upload it into S4 HANA

Please refer to the configuration steps described here 

2. Activate the AIF

TCODE: /AIF/SETUP,  switch off 'test mode'

3. Unpack AIF Content

TCODE: /AIF/CONTENT_EXTRACT

4. Ensure that the Web service Point-to-Point is active in Customizing

5. Download WSDL file from SAP Accelerator Hub

https://help.sap.com/docs/link-disclaimer?site=https%3A%2F%2Fapi.sap.com%2Fapi%2FOP_CO_SDSLS_ESR_SALES_ORDER_NOTIF%2Foverview

6. Modify WSDL

Add namespace Prefix definitions

xmlns:wsp="http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/ws/2004/09/policy"
xmlns:sp="http://docs.oasis-open.org/wss/oasis-wss-wssecurity-policy-1.2"

Add a new policy before the wsdl:type section

    <wsp:Policy wsu:Id="ClientCertificateBindingPolicy">
        <wsp:ExactlyOne>
            <wsp:All>
                <!-- Mandate mutual authentication using X.509 Certificate -->
                <sp:TransportBinding xmlns:sp="http://docs.oasis-open.org/ws-sx/ws-securitypolicy/200702">
                    <wsp:Policy>
                        <sp:TransportToken>
                            <wsp:Policy>
                                <sp:HttpsToken>
                                    <wsp:Policy>
                                        <wsp:ExactlyOne>
                                            <!-- Require Client Certificate -->
                                            <sp:RequireClientCertificate/>
                                        </wsp:ExactlyOne>
                                    </wsp:Policy>
                                </sp:HttpsToken>
                            </wsp:Policy>
                        </sp:TransportToken>
                        <sp:AlgorithmSuite>
                            <wsp:Policy>
                                <wsp:ExactlyOne>
                                    <sp:Basic256Sha256/>
                                </wsp:ExactlyOne>
                            </wsp:Policy>
                        </sp:AlgorithmSuite>
                    </wsp:Policy>
                </sp:TransportBinding>
            </wsp:All>
        </wsp:ExactlyOne>
    </wsp:Policy>

add the following policy reference inside wsdl:binding section

        <wsp:Policy>
            <wsp:PolicyReference URI="#ClientCertificateBindingPolicy"/>
        </wsp:Policy>

Update target endpoint

For example, if this is the service endpoint to be consumed:

https://api.example.com:50001/v1/users

 find out below string in the WSDL and replace it

https://host:port/

This is how the WSDL looks like after the modification

7. Create Profile

choose Technical Administration —> Profile

create a name, say Client_Cert

select SSL, then choose client certification

activate the profile

8. Create Provider System

choose Technical Administration  -> Provider Systems.

choose 'create special' , then 'create Third Party System'

input a system name and select the profile defined just now

input the username password for the SAP S4HANA system

DO NOT activate the system. Just save it. 

Open the system in change mode

go to tab WSDL Assignment. choose to upload the WSDL modified.

After uploading the WSDL,  you can save and activate the provider system.

Check connection, you should get this view

9. Maintain 'Logon Data'

click create button

fill in a name which does make sense for future reading

choose User/Password or X.509, select the PSE maintained with .pfx file

activate it.

10. Local Configuration

go to Service Administration  --> Local Integration Scenario Configuration

click save button

Fill up a name which does make sense

Under Service Definitions, add the inbound service SalesOrderBulkRequest_In and assign the profile you created earlier.

Next step, As the service group, choose  SDSLS_A2A_SO_NOTIF

With this service group, you add Sales Order (A2A) and Sales Order - Send Processing Notification (A2A).

For the provider IBC reference, choose the provider system that you created earlier.

select the logon data maintain just now in the next step

confirm and activate the configuration

11. Process Tasks

choose Service Administration ->  Pending Tasks

click process task List

if everything has configured correctly , you should get a view similar to this:

 

12. Check the Logical Port generated

goto ‘Web Service Configuration', search for ‘SDSLS_A2A_SO_NOTIF’

there should be logical port generated with a numberic id

in the security tab, it should be similar to this view

in the transport setting tab, you should see the replacing URL modified to WSDL filled in the URL field

Since the logical port is via local configuration or automatically configured, none of the field is editable. This is a big contrast to the manual configuration. 

Conclusion

1. By using OpenSSL, you should be able to generate the .pfx file which uploaded into SAP S4

2. By modifing WSDL, you should be able to generate the client certificate authentication configured in the logical port.

Reference

https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/ws-local-configuration..html

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blog-posts-by-members/service-group-configuration-in-soamanager-using-ssl-certificates/ba-p/13572482

entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I am currently developing a RAP-based Fiori application and have a requirement to update data from a custom table in the UI to a notification using the standard function module IQS4_ADD_DATA_NOTIFICATION.

I added a custom button on the UI that retrieves data from the selected line and then calls this function module to perform the update. However, based on RAP architecture, function modules or BAPIs that contain explicit COMMIT WORK or ROLLBACK WORK statements are not allowed. When triggering the action, I encounter a BEHAVIOR_ILLEGAL_STATEMENT dump.

I am aware of possible approaches such as using a background job or bgPF (background processing framework). However, I am looking for guidance on the most suitable and recommended solution for this specific scenario

Has anyone faced a similar issue or found a suitable workaround for this scenario in RAP?

I would appreciate any guidance or best practices.

Thank you,

entry.title: Custom Business Configurations (F4579): Collaborative draft
entry.content:
Introduction

The Custom Business Configurations (CUBCO) app serves as an entry point to the Business Configuration Maintenance Object (SMBC) provided by custom applications or partners.

When you use the ADT wizard to generate a Business Configuration Maintenance Object, the singleton pattern is used when generating the draft enabled RAP BO. One attribute of this pattern is that all entities are locked, so it is not possible for multiple users to work with the same configuration at the same time.

This blog describes how to avoid this restriction by using collaborative drafts.

This blog is relevant for

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 
  • SAP BTP ABAP environment 
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 

Further reading:

  • Related blog posts
  • Learn how you can use ABAP technology to develop innovative applications and business solutions across SAP’s portfolio on SAP Learning Site.

Example scenario

In the configuration table ZDEMO_CC, the company codes are stored.

@EndUserText.label : 'Company Code'
@AbapCatalog.enhancement.category : #NOT_EXTENSIBLE
@AbapCatalog.tableCategory : #TRANSPARENT
@AbapCatalog.deliveryClass : #C
@AbapCatalog.dataMaintenance : #ALLOWED
define table zdemo_cc {
  key client            : abap.clnt not null;
  key company_code      : abap.numc(4) not null;
  country               : land1;
  last_changed_at       : abp_lastchange_tstmpl;
  local_last_changed_at : abp_locinst_lastchange_tstmpl;
}

Enable collaborative draft

In the generated behavior definition, add "collaborative" to the draft annotation:

managed with additional save implementation in class ZBP_I_COMPANYCODE_S unique;
strict;
with collaborative draft;

Add the draft query annotation and the share action to the singleton entity definition:

define behavior for ZI_CompanyCode_S alias CompanyCodeAll
draft table ZDEMO_CC_D_S query ZR_DEMO_CC_CD
[...]
{
  draft action Share;
  [...]
}

 Right-click the singleton draft table and choose New Data Definition to create the CDS view for the draft query:

@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'Draft Query View'
define view entity ZR_DEMO_CC_CD
  as select from zdemo_cc_d_s
{
  key singletonid                   as Singletonid,
      lastchangedatmax              as Lastchangedatmax,
      transportrequestid            as Transportrequestid,
      draftentitycreationdatetime   as Draftentitycreationdatetime,
      draftentitylastchangedatetime as Draftentitylastchangedatetime,
      draftadministrativedatauuid   as Draftadministrativedatauuid,
      draftentityoperationcode      as Draftentityoperationcode,
      hasactiveentity               as Hasactiveentity,
      draftfieldchanges             as Draftfieldchanges
}

You need to enhance the authorization of the involved users. In SAP BTP ABAP environment and SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition you assign business catalog SAP_CORE_BC_RAP_DRAFT_PC to the user, in SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition authorization object S_DRAFT.

User TB is creating a new company code 400 while at the same time user PW is editing company code 100. All changes are recorded on the same transport request.

Since the collaborative draft is enabled for the singleton entity, the save and discard draft action are applied to all changes. In practice, this means that discarding the draft or saving must be coordinated between the users involved.
For completely independent editing of individual objects, you must develop a RAP BO without a singleton pattern.

entry.title: Re: BtchStgLoc - LC when this mrp element will be created...
entry.content:
In MD04 (Stock/Requirements List), every line you see is an MRP element. These elements are created automatically by the system when certain transactions or planning activities occur. The descriptions you saw in table T457T are simply the text labels for those element codes (like DepReq, BStLoc, StLoc, etc.)

Here is how the MRP elements get created:

Dependent Requirements (DepReq) → Generated when a higher‑level BOM component is planned. For example, if a finished good requires a raw material, MRP creates a dependent requirement for that raw material.

Purchase Requisitions (PRqRel, PurRqs) → Created when MRP runs and determines external procurement is needed.

Planned Orders (PlOrd) → Created when MRP decides in‑house production is required.

Stock Transfer Requisitions / Batch Storage Location (BStLoc) → Created when MRP determines stock needs to be moved between plants or storage locations.

Sales Orders (OrdRes) → Appear when a customer order is entered in SD; they consume available stock.

Reservations (ResReq) → Created when a production order or maintenance order reserves material.

Schedule Lines (SchLne) → Created when scheduling agreements are in place.

So, the MRP element you saw in MD04 (for example, BStLoc) was created because MRP determined that material had to be transferred from one storage location or batch to another. The system automatically generates that requirement during the planning run or when a transaction (like a sales order or production order) triggers it.

entry.title: Re: Medical device stock to clinic and traceability for patient billing
entry.content: Hi Kartik, This is a very relevant scenario in the medical device industry, especially where reuse, traceability, and service-based billing are involved. Instead of going with a full consignment model (which can become quite complex to manage across multiple clinics), a better approach is to treat the device as a loaner or equipment-based model. You can send the device using a free-of-charge or loan delivery in SAP SD while maintaining strict serial number tracking, and avoid billing at this stage since revenue is generated from the service, not the product itself. This keeps the process lightweight while still maintaining control and compliance. When the device is returned, you can process it through a return delivery and update its lifecycle status (for example: in use → returned → under processing → available → scrapped). It is highly recommended to integrate this with SAP PM (Plant Maintenance) or serial number management so each device is treated like an equipment asset. This allows you to track usage history, number of cycles, and ensure proper compliance (especially important for FDA-regulated environments). In fact, many manufacturers working on reusable and high-precision medical components—like AK Stamping—focus heavily on engineering accuracy, traceability, and ISO-certified processes to meet strict medical standards. For billing, since the actual revenue comes from the patient data/report, the ideal approach is to create a service order after the device is returned and data is downloaded. This service order should link the device serial number, hospital/clinic, and patient (via a reference or custom field). Billing can then be triggered using standard service billing methods (like resource-related billing or SD service billing). The key here is to maintain a strong mapping between device serial number, patient, and customer, ensuring full traceability and audit readiness. Overall, a hybrid approach using SD for logistics and PM/CS for tracking and service processing works best. It keeps operations simple while ensuring compliance, reuse tracking, and accurate service-based billing.

entry.title: Aprender Jugando: El Poder de la Simulación SAP ERPsim en el Aula > By ERPsim Lab - HEC Montréal
entry.content:
Página de registro: Aprender Jugando: El Poder de la Simulación SAP ERPsim en el Aula Tickets, Tuesday, May 12 from 2 pm to 5 pm CEST | Eventbrite
 
 
Overview
Masterclass SAP ERPsim: Claves de Implementación. Taller en español impartido por la profesora Andrea Ollo, UPNA, España

Descripción del Evento

¿Buscas una forma real de conectar la teoría empresarial con la práctica profesional? Te invitamos a este taller exclusivo diseñado para docentes que desean dar el salto a la gamificación con SAP y ERPsim.

Bajo la guía de la Prof. Andrea Ollo, experta certificada en ERPsim, descubrirás cómo las simulaciones de negocio en tiempo real pueden aumentar drásticamente el compromiso y el aprendizaje de tus estudiantes.

¿Qué aprenderás en estos 90 minutos?

  • El ecosistema ERPsim: Entenderás por qué es la herramienta líder en simulación empresarial SAP a nivel mundial con foco en el ERPsim Manufacutring Game.
  • Dinámica de Juego: Cómo gestionar una empresa virtual en tiempo real .
  • Valor Académico: Estrategias para integrar la simulación en tus guías docentes y evaluar competencias clave.
  • Primeros pasos: Todo lo que necesitas saber para lanzar tu primera simulación con éxito.

Estructura del Programa

Sesión Opcional (Infomación, Introducción): 28 de abril, 14:00 – 15:30 (CET).

  • Sesión dirigida a profesores que no han utilizado simulaciones de negocios o SAP previamente en sus clases con el objetivo de garantizar que todos los participantes comiencen el taller principal con la misma base técnica.

Taller Principal: 12 de mayo de 2026 | 14:00 – 17:00 (CET).

  • Sesión intensiva de 3 horas de inmersión total.

Información Clave

  • Ponente: Prof.  Andrea Ollo (Instructor Certificado ERPsim Nivel 3).
  • Alcance: Dirigido a profesores y académicos de la región Iberia.
  • Requisitos: No es necesaria experiencia previa en SAP.
  • Patrocinio / Con la colaboración de: SAP Academic Partnerships y ERPsim Lab, HEC Montréal

Acerca de Andrea Ollo

Andrea Ollo López (Pamplona, España) es profesora titular en el Departamento de Gestión de Empresas de la Universidad Pública de Navarra (UPNA). Es licenciada en Administración y Dirección de Empresas por la UPNA y doctora en el programa interuniversitario “Doctorate in Economics, Management and Organization (Business Economics)”.

Su actividad docente e investigadora se centra en el área de Organización de Empresas, con especial interés en la gestión de recursos humanos y el papel de las personas como factor clave de competitividad. Ha impartido docencia en grado y máster, y cuenta con una amplia experiencia en la dirección de trabajos académicos y participación en proyectos de investigación nacionales e internacionales.

En el ámbito de la innovación docente, la profesora Andrea está especializada en el uso de simuladores empresariales, particularmente en entornos SAP. Es formadora certificada como Train the Trainer (TTT) de ERPsim, y cuenta con experiencia en la implementación del simulador ERPsim Manufacturing Game.

entry.title: Re: Designing File Processing in BTP Integration Suite with Amazon S3 and Apache POI
entry.content:
Excellent blog. 

Our SAP Integration Suite (Cloud Integration) tenant is running on AWS Frankfurt, and I noticed that when provisioning the Object Store service in SAP BTP, it creates an Amazon S3 bucket behind the scenes.

I wanted to understand the practical usage of this in integration scenarios.

  1. Can the BTP Object Store (S3 bucket) be used as an alternative to a traditional SFTP server for file-based integrations in CPI?

  2. If yes, how do external systems typically upload/download files to this bucket (API, AWS CLI, pre-signed URLs, etc.)?

  3. Is there any way to expose this bucket through SFTP protocol, or would that require additional services like AWS Transfer Family?

  4. Are there any recommended best practices or limitations when using S3/Object Store instead of SFTP for CPI integrations?

  5. How does the pricing work for Object Store in SAP BTP?

    • Is there any free storage included with the Integration Suite subscription? our CPI license is subscription based not consumption based.
    • Or is it pay-as-you-use similar to AWS S3?

Would appreciate insights from anyone who has implemented this pattern in production.

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: 2ET: Correct CBC Org Structure for Sales Org / Plant...
entry.content: Hello Lakshmipathi, thank you, your hint was correct. The root cause was in the relations of Plant DE01: “Plant to Sales Organization and Distribution Channel” was not assigned to Sales Organization DE01. Because of that, DE01 was visible in Organizational Structure, but it did not appear in “Assign Sales Organization – Distribution Channel – Plant.” I corrected the missing relation, reconfirmed the structure, deployed the change, and DE01 is now visible correctly in the assignment: DE01 / DE / DE01. Best regards, Tobias

entry.title: Transaction cannot be revived after delayed abortion(RAP_RUNTIME 019) - Must ROLLBACK After Failures
entry.content:
1. Symptom (ST22 / Dump Keywords)

A typical dump looks like this (same category as in your case):

  • Runtime Error: RAISE_SHORTDUMP
  • Exception: CX_SADL_DUMP_APPL_MODEL_ERROR
  • T100: RAP_RUNTIME / 019
  • Short Text: Transaction cannot be revived after delayed abortion (BO: I_ENTERPRISEPROJECTTP_2)

When debugging you may also notice RAP internal state like:

  • CL_RAP_BHV_PROCESSOR->IF_RAP_LEGACY_TRANSACTION~MV_ABORT = abap_true

Meaning: RAP has already marked the logical transaction as aborted (delayed abortion). It must not be reused.

2. Root Cause: RAP Transaction State + Delayed Abortion

A RAP EML “logical transaction” typically consists of two phases:

  1. Transaction phase
  • MODIFY ENTITIES runs validations/determinations/checks.
  • Errors are usually returned in FAILED / REPORTED
  1. Save phase
  • COMMIT ENTITIES triggers the save sequence (finalize/save, etc.).
  • Errors again come back via FAILED / REPORTED (in the commit response this is the LATE response).

If anything fails in either phase, RAP can enter delayed abortion:
Your ABAP code continues running,but the RAP logical transaction becomes unusable.
Any subsequent EML using that aborted transaction context may lead to RAP_RUNTIME 019.

MODIFY ENTITIES OF <BO> ... FAILED ls_failed_tx REPORTED ls_reported_tx.
COMMIT ENTITIES ... FAILED ls_failed_save REPORTED ls_reported_save.

" If commit failed but you don't rollback here,
" next EML call can dump (RAP_RUNTIME 019)
MODIFY ENTITIES OF <BO> ...

Key point: Never continue with another EML step after a failure without rollback.

5. Correct Example Using I_EnterpriseProjectTP_3

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& Report zdemo_rap_eml_epp3
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*&
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
REPORT zdemo_rap_eml_epp3.

PARAMETERS:
      p_uuid TYPE sysuuid_c36 OBLIGATORY,
      p_desc TYPE string      DEFAULT 'Demo update from EML'.

START-OF-SELECTION.

  DATA lv_uuid_x16 TYPE sysuuid_x16.

  TRY.
              cl_system_uuid=>convert_uuid_c36_static(
                  EXPORTING
                      uuid     = p_uuid
                  IMPORTING
                      uuid_x16 = lv_uuid_x16 ).
        CATCH cx_uuid_error.
              WRITE: / 'Invalid UUID format. Use 36-char UUID (xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx).'.
              RETURN.
  ENDTRY.

  DATA:
        ls_failed_tx     TYPE RESPONSE FOR FAILED   i_enterpriseprojecttp_3,
        ls_reported_tx   TYPE RESPONSE FOR REPORTED i_enterpriseprojecttp_3,
        ls_failed_save   TYPE RESPONSE FOR FAILED   LATE i_enterpriseprojecttp_3,
        ls_reported_save TYPE RESPONSE FOR REPORTED LATE i_enterpriseprojecttp_3.

  TRY.
              " Transaction phase
              MODIFY ENTITIES OF i_enterpriseprojecttp_3
                  ENTITY EnterpriseProject
                  UPDATE FIELDS ( ProjectDescription )
                  WITH VALUE #(
                      ( %key-ProjectUUID      = lv_uuid_x16
                          ProjectDescription    = p_desc )
                  )
                  FAILED   ls_failed_tx
                  REPORTED ls_reported_tx.

              IF ls_failed_tx IS NOT INITIAL.
                    WRITE: / 'MODIFY ENTITIES failed (transaction phase) -> ROLLBACK ENTITIES'.
                    ROLLBACK ENTITIES.
                    RETURN.
              ENDIF.

              " Save phase
              COMMIT ENTITIES
                  BEGIN RESPONSE OF i_enterpriseprojecttp_3
                  FAILED   ls_failed_save
                  REPORTED ls_reported_save.
              COMMIT ENTITIES END.

              IF ls_failed_save IS NOT INITIAL.
                    WRITE: / 'COMMIT ENTITIES failed (save phase) -> ROLLBACK ENTITIES'.
                    ROLLBACK ENTITIES.
                    RETURN.
              ENDIF.

              WRITE: / 'COMMIT ENTITIES OK.'.

        CATCH cx_root INTO DATA(lx_root).
              WRITE: / 'Unexpected exception:', lx_root->get_text( ).
              " Keep the EML contract: if anything goes wrong, end the logical transaction.
              ROLLBACK ENTITIES.
  ENDTRY.

Two important details in this example

  1. COMMIT ENTITIES requires LATE response types
  • RESPONSE FOR FAILED LATE i_enterpriseprojecttp_3
  • RESPONSE FOR REPORTED LATE i_enterpriseprojecttp_3
  1. “Fail fast” with rollback
  • If FAILED is not initial in either phase, rollback and end the logical transaction.
  • Start the next business step in a new logical transaction context.

 

8. FAQ: Why not ROLLBACK WORK?

In RAP EML, use the EML-compatible statement:

  • Errors in EML logical transaction → ROLLBACK ENTITIES

ROLLBACK WORK is lower-level LUW handling and can lead to semantic mismatches with RAP’s logical transaction handling. The dump you saw is fundamentally about the RAP logical transaction being aborted and still being reused.

entry.title: Re: File content in ASCII and file characterset in EBCDIC
entry.content:
According to the SAP Multi‑Bank Connectivity standard behavior, SAP Multi‑Bank Connectivity does not perform character set conversion for inbound or outbound files.

SAP Multi‑Bank Connectivity acts as a secure transport, routing, and validation layer. Incoming files are technically validated, including file structure and character encoding. If a file is delivered with a character set of EBCDIC, it will be rejected, even if the visible content appears to be ASCII.

There is no configuration option in SAP Multi‑Bank Connectivity to:

  • Ignore the character set
  • Automatically convert EBCDIC to ASCII / UTF‑8
  • Remap character encoding during processing

This is intentional and by design, as SAP Multi‑Bank Connectivity does not perform file transformations.

Recommended resolution

  • The bank must provide files in a supported character encoding (for example ASCII or UTF‑8), as agreed in the interface definition.
  • If EBCDIC is used on the bank side, the conversion must happen before the file reaches SAP Multi‑Bank Connectivity, for example:
    • On the bank side, or
    • Via an external middleware / preprocessing layer outside SAP Multi‑Bank Connectivity

Once the file reaches SAP Multi‑Bank Connectivity, character set conversion is not supported.

Relevant SAP documentation

  • SAP Multi‑Bank Connectivity – Security Capabilities
    https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_MULTI_BANK_CONNECTIVITY/security-capabilities

  • SAP Multi‑Bank Connectivity to Bank / FSI
    https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_MULTI_BANK_CONNECTIVITY/sap-multi-bank-connectivity-to-bank-fsi

  • SFTP (H2H) Integration – SAP Multi‑Bank Connectivity
    https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_MULTI_BANK_CONNECTIVITY/sftp-h2h

Summary:
SAP Multi‑Bank Connectivity cannot ignore or convert EBCDIC character sets. Files must be delivered in a supported encoding; otherwise, they will be rejected.

entry.title: S/4 HANA How to Get rid of Basic Auth - SOAMANAGER Local Configuration on Client Certificate
entry.content:
The local configuration in SOAMANAGER is also called automatic configuration. Rather than configured manully, the logical port is configured by the task automatically in the background.

However, in this mode, the authentication doesn't support OAuth2. To get rid of basic auth, an option is to use client certificate.

Configuration Steps

let's take sales order notification outbound as an example.

This is the office help document:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/19d48293097f4a2589433856b034dfa5/ed66f965148548a9bb76c24be640d7fc.html?locale=en-US

1. Generate certificate files and upload it into S4 HANA

Please refer to the configuration steps described here 

2. Activate the AIF

TCODE: /AIF/SETUP,  switch off 'test mode'

3. Unpack AIF Content

TCODE: /AIF/CONTENT_EXTRACT

4. Ensure that the Web service Point-to-Point is active in Customizing

5. Download WSDL file from SAP Accelerator Hub

https://help.sap.com/docs/link-disclaimer?site=https%3A%2F%2Fapi.sap.com%2Fapi%2FOP_CO_SDSLS_ESR_SALES_ORDER_NOTIF%2Foverview

6. Modify WSDL

Add namespace Prefix definitions

xmlns:wsp="http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/ws/2004/09/policy"
xmlns:sp="http://docs.oasis-open.org/wss/oasis-wss-wssecurity-policy-1.2"

Add a new policy before the wsdl:type section

    <wsp:Policy wsu:Id="ClientCertificateBindingPolicy">
        <wsp:ExactlyOne>
            <wsp:All>
                <!-- Mandate mutual authentication using X.509 Certificate -->
                <sp:TransportBinding xmlns:sp="http://docs.oasis-open.org/ws-sx/ws-securitypolicy/200702">
                    <wsp:Policy>
                        <sp:TransportToken>
                            <wsp:Policy>
                                <sp:HttpsToken>
                                    <wsp:Policy>
                                        <wsp:ExactlyOne>
                                            <!-- Require Client Certificate -->
                                            <sp:RequireClientCertificate/>
                                        </wsp:ExactlyOne>
                                    </wsp:Policy>
                                </sp:HttpsToken>
                            </wsp:Policy>
                        </sp:TransportToken>
                        <sp:AlgorithmSuite>
                            <wsp:Policy>
                                <wsp:ExactlyOne>
                                    <sp:Basic256Sha256/>
                                </wsp:ExactlyOne>
                            </wsp:Policy>
                        </sp:AlgorithmSuite>
                    </wsp:Policy>
                </sp:TransportBinding>
            </wsp:All>
        </wsp:ExactlyOne>
    </wsp:Policy>

add the following policy reference inside wsdl:binding section

        <wsp:Policy>
            <wsp:PolicyReference URI="#ClientCertificateBindingPolicy"/>
        </wsp:Policy>

Update target endpoint

For example, if this is the service endpoint to be consumed:

https://api.example.com:50001/v1/users

 find out below string in the WSDL and replace it

https://host:port/

This is how the WSDL looks like after the modification

7. Create Profile

choose Technical Administration —> Profile

create a name, say Client_Cert

select SSL, then choose client certification

activate the profile

8. Create Provider System

choose Technical Administration  -> Provider Systems.

choose 'create special' , then 'create Third Party System'

input a system name and select the profile defined just now

input the username password for the SAP S4HANA system

DO NOT activate the system. Just save it. 

Open the system in change mode

go to tab WSDL Assignment. choose to upload the WSDL modified.

After uploading the WSDL,  you can save and activate the provider system.

Check connection, you should get this view

9. Maintain 'Logon Data'

click create button

fill in a name which does make sense for future reading

choose User/Password or X.509, select the PSE maintained with .pfx file

activate it.

10. Local Configuration

go to Service Administration  --> Local Integration Scenario Configuration

click save button

Fill up a name which does make sense

Under Service Definitions, add the inbound service SalesOrderBulkRequest_In and assign the profile you created earlier.

Next step, As the service group, choose  SDSLS_A2A_SO_NOTIF

With this service group, you add Sales Order (A2A) and Sales Order - Send Processing Notification (A2A).

For the provider IBC reference, choose the provider system that you created earlier.

select the logon data maintain just now in the next step

confirm and activate the configuration

11. Process Tasks

choose Service Administration ->  Pending Tasks

click process task List

if everything has configured correctly , you should get a view similar to this:

 

12. Check the Logical Port generated

goto ‘Web Service Configuration', search for ‘SDSLS_A2A_SO_NOTIF’

there should be logical port generated with a numberic id

in the security tab, it should be similar to this view

in the transport setting tab, you should see the replacing URL modified to WSDL filled in the URL field

Since the logical port is via local configuration or automatically configured, none of the field is editable. This is a big contrast to the manual configuration. 

Conclusion

1. By using OpenSSL, you should be able to generate the .pfx file which uploaded into SAP S4

2. By modifing WSDL, you should be able to generate the client certificate authentication configured in the logical port.

Reference

https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/ws-local-configuration..html

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blog-posts-by-members/service-group-configuration-in-soamanager-using-ssl-certificates/ba-p/13572482

entry.title: S/4 HANA How to Get rid of Basic Auth
entry.content:
Basic authentication (Auth) is inherently insecure because it transmits credentials in base64-encoded cleartext with every request, making them vulnerable to interception without HTTPS. It lacks session management, logout functionality, and support for multifactor authentication (MFA), making it vulnerable to replay and brute-force attacks.

Here are Key Disadvantages of Basic Auth:

  • Credential Exposure: Username and password are sent in every request. If not using HTTPS, these are trivially captured and decoded, as Base64 is encoding, not encryption.
  • Replay Attacks: Because credentials are sent repeatedly, a captured header allows an attacker to replay the request and gain access until the password changes.
  • Lack of Session Control: There is no native logout or session expiration mechanism. Once credentials are leaked, they remain valid indefinitely until the password is changed.
  • Poor Security Controls: Basic Auth often lacks support for modern security features, including rate-limiting, account lockout after failed attempts, and multi-factor authentication (MFA).
  • Poor User Experience: Browsers often cache credentials until closed, making it difficult for users to sign out or switch accounts without closing the browser.

While it is easy to implement for internal tools, its lack of security makes it unsuitable for production environments or systems containing sensitive data, especially when the connection is over internet.

Here is my experience how to get rid of basic auth for S/4HANA in difference connection types, without middleware layer. For Inbound/Outbound IDOC connection without PI/CPI as the middleware, a Jco server should be deployed to derive IDOC into an industry standard format, like XML. This design does support client certificate. However since IDOC + Jco is a bit old communication combination, I am not going to address it in a separated postal. Please refer to the web service on client certificate.

The blog post is more for the integration developer rather than the UI developer.

When S/4 HANA works as a service provider(server)

  • API based on OAuth
  • SOAP Inbound web service on the client certificate

When S/4 HANA works as a service consumer(client)

  • RFC Destination on OAuth2
  • RFC Destination on Client Certificate
  • SOAMANAGER Manual Configuration on Oauth2
  • SOAMANAGER Local Configuration on Client Certificate
